name: Dahhak al-Mashriqi
title: Conditional Companion
shortDescription: Someone who stayed in Karbala on the condition of saving his life, fought until the afternoon of Ashura, and escaped with the Imam's permission to become a narrator of history.
fullStory:
Dahhak ibn Abdullah al-Mashriqi is one of the most unusual and distinct attendees of Karbala. He met Imam Hussain (AS) along the way. The Imam invited him to help him.

Dahhak said: 'I will stay on the condition that I fight as long as my help is beneficial to you, but whenever I see that my death brings no benefit to you, I have permission to leave.' Imam Hussain (AS) magnanimously accepted this condition.

On the day of Ashura, Dahhak fought bravely and killed several enemies. He even prayed the noon prayer of Ashura with the Imam. But in the afternoon of Ashura, when he saw that all the Imam's (AS) companions had been martyred and the enemy had tightened the siege, he went to the Imam and reminded him of his condition.

The Imam said: 'Yes, I leave you free, but how will you escape?' Dahhak mounted his horse, which he had hidden beforehand, and quickly escaped through the Kufan army. He survived and later became one of the most accurate narrators of the events of Karbala in history.
quoteArabic: يا ضَحّاكُ! كَيْفَ لَكَ بِالنَّجاءِ؟... فَشَأْنُكَ...
quote: (The Imam said to him:) O Dahhak! How will you save yourself? If you can, go, you are free.
